contents:
# Former INDOPACOM Intelligence Chief Urges Taiwan to Pass Massive Defense Budget

## Overview
Former U.S. Indo-Pacific Command intelligence chief Rear Adm. Michael Studeman called on Taiwan's Legislature to approve a NT$1.25 trillion supplementary defense budget immediately. He emphasized preparing defenses beyond 2026 amid ongoing Chinese threats, dismissing short-term political delays.[2]

## Details
Studeman referenced the "Davidson window" of potential Chinese invasion capability by 2027, despite recent U.S. intelligence assessments. He highlighted China's large-scale encirclement exercises and gray-zone harassment as reasons for vigilance. The interview occurred Wednesday with CNA in Taipei. Studeman authored a book predicting a possible Chinese attack on Taiwan this decade. He stressed Taiwan must not let "temporary headwinds" derail strategy.[2]

**Location**: Taipei, Taiwan

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @ThePacificBrief, which covers Indo-Pacific defense intelligence PLA Taiwan analysis. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Indo-Pacific defense intelligence PLA Taiwan analysis will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
